name: Yogurt
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Commercial yogurt (or 1 package of Yogurt starter)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the milk into a 2-quart pan and sprinkle the gelatin on top.
2. Let soak for about 5 minutes, then stir with a wire whisk while heating to almost boiling (to pasteurize).
3. Cool to warm. Add the commercial yogurt (unflavored) and whisk in until incorporated.
4. Rinse small jars with hot water and fill with the warm yogurt mixture (1/2 pint canning jars work well for this and are approximately the size of a serving from the store).
5. Set in a warm place overnight, covered with a blanket.
6. Alternatively, you can pour the yogurt mix into a warmed thermos bottle and let it sit overnight. Personally, I use the Salton Yogurt maker found at the Salvation Army for $2.00; it works every time.
